sections.paragraph:
παντοκράτωρ, ορος, ὁ (πᾶς, κρατέω; Kaibel 815 s. below; Porphyr., Philos. Ex. Orac. ed. GWolff 1856, p. 145 ln. 157=Theosophien 27 p. 174, 4; SEG VIII, 548, 2 [I b.c.], the fem.; CIG 2569, 12; Sb 4127, 19 of the Egypt. sun-god Mandulis; of Hermes PGM 7, 668; likew. Kaibel 815, 11 [II a.d.] π. Ἐριούνιε [Hermes]; PMich 155, 3 [II a.d.]; PLips 40 II, 13; PGM 4, 272; 969 [s. New Docs 3, 118]; HGraillot, Les Dieux tout-puissants, Cybèle et Attis: RevArch 58, 1904 I 331ff; Cumont3 230f.—Much more freq. in Jewish [LXX.—S. ZTalshir, JQR 78, ’87, 57–75; TestSol, TestAbr, ParJer; GrBar 1:3; EpArist, Philo; Sib-Or 1, 66 and Fgm. 1, 8.—Not in Joseph.] and Christian wr.: Iren. 1, 9, 2 [Harv. I 82, 4]; Theoph. Ant. 2, 3 [p. 100, 1]. π. of the Demiurge in gnostic speculation Iren. 1, 11, 1 [Harv. I 101, 2]) the Almighty, All-Powerful, Omnipotent (One) only of God (as transl. of צְבָאוֹת [Orig., C. Cels. 5, 45, 46] and שַׁדַּי) π. θεός (3 Macc 6:2; Just., D. 38, 2 al.; Mel., P. 45, 322) 1 Cl ins; ὁ π. θεός (2 Macc 8:18) 2:3; 32:4; 62:2; AcPl Ha 6, 14; ὁ θεὸς ὁ π. Rv 16:14; 19:15; AcPlCor 2:12. ὁ θεὸς ὁ τῶν ὅλων ὁ π. 2:9. θεὸς π. (Jer 3:19) Pol ins; AcPlCor 1:11. ὁ π. καὶ παντοκτίστης καὶ ἀόρατος θεός Dg 7:2; κύριος π. (oft. LXX) 2 Cor 6:18. (ὁ) κύριος ὁ θεὸς ὁ π. (=י״י אֱלֹהֵי הַצְּבָאוֹת.—Hos 12:6; Am 3:13; 4:13; 5:14) Rv 1:8; 4:8; 11:17; 15:3; 16:7; 21:22; MPol 14:1; κύριος ὁ θεὸς ἡμῶν ὁ π. Rv 19:6; ὁ θεὸς καὶ πατὴρ π. MPol 19:2 (cp. Just., D. 139, 4). God is addressed in eucharistic prayer as δέσποτα π. D 10:3 (cp. 3 Macc 2:2 μόναρχε παντοκράτωρ).—νουθέτημα παντοκράτορος 1 Cl 56:6 (Job 5:17).—We find the gen. and dat. (sing.), which is the same in all genders, used w. the neut. ὄνομα. This becomes possible (s. παντοκρατορικός) because of the fact that God’s name is almost equivalent to God’s being (s. ὄνομα 1d). τῷ ῥήματι τοῦ παντοκράτορος καὶ ἐνδόξου ὀνόματος Hv 3, 3, 5. The ms. rdg. τῷ παντοκράτορι καὶ παναρέτῳ ὀνόματί σου 1 Cl 60:4 (s. παντοκρατορικός) is more difficult, since here the name and God are separated by σου.—FKattenbusch, Das apostolische Symbol II 1900, 520ff; Dodd 19; HHommel, TLZ 79, ’54, 283f. Pantokrator: Theologia Viatorum 5, ’53/54; OMontevecchi, Studi in Onore di ACalderini e RParibeni II, ’56, 401–32.—DELG s.v. πᾶς. M-M. DDD ‘Almighty’ 36–41. EDNT. TW. Sv.

Kaibel Kaibel = Epigrammata Graeca ex Lapidibus Conlecta, ed. GK.—List 3

  1. ed. = edited (by), edition
  2. ln. = line

Theosophien Theosophien = Fragmente griechischer Theosophien, dates uncertain—List 5

SEG SEG = Supplementum Epigraphicum Graecum—List 3

fem. fem. = feminine

CIG CIG = Corpus Inscriptionum Graecarum—List 3

Sb Sb = Sammelbuch griechischer Urkunden aus Aegypten—Lists 3, 4

PGM PGM = Papyri Graecae Magicae—List 4

likew. likew. = likewise

PMich PMich = Michigan Papyri—List 4

PLips PLips = Griech. Urkunden der Papyrussammlung zu Leipzig—List 4

RevArch RevArch = Revue Archéologique—List 6

Cumont Cumont = FC., Die orientalische Religionen im römischen Heidentum—List 6

freq. freq. = frequent(ly)

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

JQR JQR = Jewish Quarterly Review—List 6

TestSol TestSol = Testament of Solomon, I–III a.d.—List 2

TestAbr TestAbr = Testament of Abraham, with some interpolations; I b.c./I a.d.—Lists 2, 5

ParJer ParJer = Paraleipomena Jeremiou, II? a.d.—List 2

GrBar GrBar = Greek Baruch (Apocalypsis Baruchi Graece)—List 2

EpArist EpArist = Epistle of Aristeas, II b.c.—List 5

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Fgm. Fgm. = fragment, fragmentary

Joseph Joseph This abbr. used when no specific texts are cited (s. Jos.); I a.d.—List 5

  1. wr. = writers, writings

Iren. Iren. = Irenaeus, Haereses, II a.d.—List 5

Harv. Harv. = WHarvey; s. Iren.—List 5

Theoph. Ant. Theoph. Ant. = Theophilus Antiochenus, II a.d.—List 5

transl. transl. = translate, translation

Orig Orig , var. works, II–III a.d.—List 5

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

Mel. Mel. = Melito of Sardis, II a.d.—List 5

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

AcPl Ha AcPl Ha = Acts of Paul, PHamb—List 1

AcPlCor AcPlCor = Acts of Paul: correspondence with Corinthians (a.k.a. Third Corinthians), text according to PBodmer X—List 1

Pol Pol = Polycarp to the Philippians—List 1

Dg Dg = Letter of Diognetus, attributed to an unidentifiable apologist; II a.d.—List 1

oft. oft. = often

MPol MPol = Martyrdom of Polycarp; after II a.d.—List 1

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

gen. gen. = genitive

dat. dat. = dative

sing. sing. = singular

neut. neut. = neuter (used when “n.” would be ambiguous)

Hv Hv = Visions

  1. ms. = manuscript(s)

rdg. rdg. = reading(s)

Dodd Dodd = CD., The Bible and the Greeks—List 6

TLZ TLZ = Theolog. Literaturzeitung—List 6

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

DDD DDD = Dictionary of Deities and Demons in the Bible—List 6

EDNT EDNT = Exegetical Dictionary of the NT—List 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Sv Sv (at the end of entries)=HSieben, Voces—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 755
